title: DTD Objects
text: DTD Objects A Uniface DTD object defines a Uniface-compliant Document Type Definition (DTD), which defines the structure of an XML stream that is used to transfer data between components. It also includes a default mapping from the elements defined in the DTD to Uniface entities and fields. Uniface-compliant DTDs are used by xmlsave and xmlload when reading and writing XML parameters. For more information, see Declarative XML Handling . You can define DTDs and default mappings to Uniface data using the DTD Editor. It enables you to generate DTDs based on modeled entities and components, import DTD definitions from a file, and map elements in a DTD to Uniface entities and fields. Compiling a DTD generates a compiled .dtd file in the /resources/dtd directory. If the File Name property is defined, it also generates a text file with a .dtd file extension. Related Links Uniface-Compliant DTDs A Document Type Definition (DTD) defines rules for the structure of an XML stream. In Uniface, a DTD is a development object that includes DTD definitions and a default mapping between the XML elements defined by the DTD and Uniface component fields and entities. It can be used to define the rules for the structure of an XML stream that is exchanged between components. Working with DTDs DTD Editor The DTD Editor allows you to load, create, or edit Document Type Definitions (DTDs) for XML data, and map them to Uniface entities and fields. Was this topic helpful?
